title: rubus costatus var. arduennensis
intro: Rubus costatus var. arduennensis, also known as the Ardenne raspberry or wild raspberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the Rosaceae family and is native to the Ardenne region in Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus costatus var. arduennensis include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and protect against oxidative stress, making it a potential treatment for various health conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in jams, jellies, and other food products due to its sweet and tart flavor.
